client_payload.summary: OpenAI updated its API terms of service. New retention defaults and stricter rate limits affect production deployments using the official SDK.

packages.summary: Default 30-day prompt/completion retention was reintroduced for non-enterprise tiers, and per-org rate limits dropped by 40% for chat completions.

breaking_changes.description: Prompts and completions are retained for up to 30 days for abuse monitoring on Standard and Team tiers.
breaking_changes.developer_impact: Anything sent through chat.completions.create may be persisted off-platform for 30 days. Re-evaluate PII redaction before send.

breaking_changes.description: Per-organization TPM limits for gpt-4o reduced from 500k to 300k tokens/min.
breaking_changes.developer_impact: Existing burst-heavy ingestion jobs will start hitting 429 responses. Add retry-with-backoff and reconsider batch sizes.

recommended_actions: Audit any user-content prompts sent to openai for PII before the next deploy.
Wrap chat.completions.create calls with exponential backoff on 429.
Consider requesting a tier upgrade if sustained TPM > 250k.

packages.summary: Agent tool-use traces are now logged for safety review on Standard tier.
recommended_actions: Document the new logging behavior in your internal AI usage policy.
